do_not_merge.reason: Contradiction test: strong identity fields differ. corridor_buslane-brochure-161st-street has corridor_name '161st Street' with no limits, just a single street example, while corridor_bx6-south-bronx has corridor_name 'South Bronx Crosstown', a defined limits (Riverside Drive West to Hunts Point), and a street list including several streets (161st Street as one component). A specific street segment example is distinct from a multi-street corridor; per corridor identity rule, scope and limits differ, so they are not the same canonical identity.

suggested_rules: When one record describes a single street as an example (no defined limits, no route context) and another describes a multi-street corridor with explicit limits and route references, they are distinct identities even if they share a street name in the same borough. The example street is at most a subsegment of the corridor and should be related via subsegment_of, not merged.
suggestion.rationale: The two records share a street name (161st Street) and borough (Bronx) but differ in all other strong identity fields: corridor_name, street list, and limits. The first is a bare street example from a bus lane brochure; the second is a fully-scoped crosstown corridor serving the Bx6 route. Per the identity merge canon, contradictory strong identity fields force do_not_merge; sharing one street name alone is insufficient for merge.
